mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals that the supply side of the economy is expanding, which reduces input costs and supports corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, the Rank IC of month-over-month INDPRO growth against 20-day forward returns is below 0.03, or the average forward return following monthly INDPRO increases is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) drops below the natural rate threshold of 4.0%, the labor market becomes excessively tight, pushing up wage costs and eroding profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After a UNRATE reading below 4.0%, the median 5-day S&P 500 return is not negative, or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) drives the ex-ante real interest rate: a decline in the real rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation) reduces the discount rate on equities, lifting present values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: any regime where real rates are sufficiently variable
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly changes in real rate and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is not reliably negative (absolute Rank IC < 0.05) or the strategy of buying when real rate declines produces a Sharpe ratio below 0.2 out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: In the late-cycle expansion regime, continued strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals an overheating economy with rising unit labor costs and an imminent policy tightening, which suppresses forward equity returns. The mechanism is regime-conditional: positive in early recovery, negative in late-cycle.
failure_condition: During identified late-cycle episodes (UNRATE < 4.5%, FEDFUNDS > 2.0%), months with above-median PAYEMS growth do not produce a negative median 60-day forward return, or the hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue growth and investor confidence, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as a persistent, always-on signal.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of month-over-month RSAFS growth against 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or the strategy of buying on positive RSAFS growth fails to outperform a buy-and-hold.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1.0 million units on a monthly basis, it signals weakening residential investment and consumer wealth, which depresses economic growth and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the short 5-day horizon as markets reprice risks.
failure_condition: Following a monthly HOUST print below 1,000K, the average 5-day S&P 500 return is not negative, or fewer than 55% of such events produce a negative return over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad-based economic momentum with reinforcing supply and labor channels, providing a strong positive signal for S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as profit growth materializes.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS rise month-over-month, the median 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not positive, or the signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (successive FEDFUNDS rate increases), the cumulative tightening restricts liquidity and raises discount rates, creating a persistent headwind for equities. This regime-conditional effect is negative for forward 20-day S&P 500 returns as long as the tightening cycle continues.
failure_condition: During periods identified as tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS rate higher than 3 months prior and rising), the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative, or the median drawdown is smaller than during neutral periods.
